heading: Activities of insured State banks
label: 1831a Activities of insured State banks
notes: References in TextThe Investment Company Act of 1940, referred to in subsec. (f)(2)(A), is title I of , , which is classified generally to subchapter I (§ 80a–1 et seq.) of chapter 2D of Title 15, Commerce and Trade. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 22, 1940, ch. 68654 Stat. 789section 80a–51 of Title 15, referred to in subsec. (j)(4), was subsequently amended, and subsec. (f) of section 1831u no longer defines the terms “host State”, “home State”, and “out-of-State bank”. However, such terms are defined elsewhere in that section.Section 1831u of this title

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1831a, [24], as added , , , provided that if the applicable rate prescribed in subsec. (a) exceeded the rate a State bank would be permitted to charge in absence of that subsection, that State bank could for a business or agricultural loan of $25,000 or more, notwithstanding State law, take or charge on any evidence of debt, interest of not more than 5 per centum in excess of the discount rate in effect at the Federal Reserve Bank in the district where the bank was located, that the taking or charging of interest at a greater rate than that prescribed by subsec. (a), if knowingly done, would be deemed a forfeit of the entire interest on that particular evidence of debt, and that if such greater rate of interest had already been paid, the payor could recover twice the amount of such payment in a civil action commenced within two years of such payment, prior to repeal by , , , effective at close of .act Sept. 21, 1950, ch. 967, § 2Pub. children.label: A
children.text: the Corporation has determined that the activity would pose no significant risk to the Deposit Insurance Fund; and

children.label: B
children.text: the State bank is, and continues to be, in compliance with applicable capital standards prescribed by the appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall make a determination under paragraph (1)(A) not later than 60 days after receipt of a completed application that may be required under this subsection.

children.heading: Extension of time period
children.label: B Extension of time period
children.text: The Corporation may extend the 60-day period referred to in subparagraph (A) for not more than 30 additional days, and shall notify the applicant of any such extension.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), an insured State bank may not engage in insurance underwriting except to the extent that activity is permissible for national banks.

children.heading: Exception for certain federally reinsured crop insurance
children.label: 2 Exception for certain federally reinsured crop insurance
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, an insured State bank or any of its subsidiaries that provided insurance on or before , which was reinsured in whole or in part by the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation may continue to provide such insurance.September 30, 1991

children.heading: Equity investments by insured State banks
children.label: c Equity investments by insured State banks

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An insured State bank may not, directly or indirectly, acquire or retain any equity investment of a type that is not permissible for a national bank.

children.heading: Exception for certain subsidiaries
children.label: 2 Exception for certain subsidiaries
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not prohibit an insured State bank from acquiring or retaining an equity investment in a subsidiary of which the insured State bank is a majority owner.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: A Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, an insured State bank may invest as a limited partner in a partnership, the sole purpose of which is direct or indirect investment in the acquisition, rehabilitation, or new construction of a qualified housing project.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The aggregate of the investments of any insured State bank pursuant to this paragraph shall not exceed 2 percent of the total assets of the bank.

children.heading: Qualified housing project
children.label: i Qualified housing project
children.text: The term “qualified housing project” means residential real estate that is intended to primarily benefit lower income people throughout the period of the investment.

children.heading: Lower income
children.label: ii Lower income
children.text: The term “lower income” means income that is less than or equal to the median income based on statistics from State or Federal sources.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall require any insured State bank to divest any equity investment the retention of which is not permissible under this subsection as quickly as can be prudently done, and in any event before the end of the 5-year period beginning on .December 19, 1991

children.heading: Treatment of noncompliance during divestment
children.label: B Treatment of noncompliance during divestment
children.text: With respect to any equity investment held by any insured State bank on , which was lawfully acquired before , the bank shall be deemed not to be in violation of the prohibition in this subsection on retaining such investment so long as the bank complies with the applicable requirements established by the Corporation for divesting such investments.December 19, 1991December 19, 1991

children.heading: Subsidiaries of insured State banks
children.label: d Subsidiaries of insured State banks
A
children.text: the Corporation has determined that the activity poses no significant risk to the Deposit Insurance Fund; and

children.label: B
children.text: the bank is, and continues to be, in compliance with applicable capital standards prescribed by the appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: A Prohibition
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), no subsidiary of an insured State bank may engage in insurance underwriting except to the extent such activities are permissible for national banks.

children.heading: Continuation of existing activities
children.label: B Continuation of existing activities
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), a well-capitalized insured State bank or any of its subsidiaries that was lawfully providing insurance as principal in a State on , may continue to provide, as principal, insurance of the same type to residents of the State (including companies or partnerships incorporated in, organized under the laws of, licensed to do business in, or having an office in the State, but only on behalf of their employees resident in or property located in the State), individuals employed in the State, and any other person to whom the bank or subsidiary has provided insurance as principal, without interruption, since such person resided in or was employed in such State.November 21, 1991

children.label: i
children.text: the insured State bank was required, before , to provide title insurance as a condition of the bank’s initial chartering under State law; andJune 1, 1991

children.label: ii
children.text: control of the insured State bank has not changed since that date.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation shall make a determination under paragraph (1)(A) not later than 60 days after receipt of a completed application that may be required under this subsection.

children.heading: Extension of time period
children.label: B Extension of time period
children.text: The Corporation may extend the 60-day period referred to in subparagraph (A) for not more than 30 additional days, and shall notify the applicant of any such extension.

children.label: A
children.text: is located in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts or the State of New York or Connecticut; and

children.label: B
children.text: meets applicable consumer disclosure requirements with respect to such insurance.

children.heading: FDIC finding and action regarding risk
children.label: 2 FDIC finding and action regarding risk

children.heading: Finding
children.label: A Finding
children.text: Before the end of the 1-year period beginning on , the Corporation shall make a finding whether savings bank life insurance activities of insured banks pose or may pose any significant risk to the Deposit Insurance Fund.December 19, 1991

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Corporation shall, pursuant to any finding made under subparagraph (A), take appropriate actions to address any risk that exists or may subsequently develop with respect to insured banks described in paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Authorized actions
children.label: ii Authorized actions
children.text: Actions the Corporation may take under this subparagraph include requiring the modification, suspension, or termination of insurance activities conducted by any insured bank if the Corporation finds that the activities pose a significant risk to any insured bank described in paragraph (1)(A) or to the Deposit Insurance Fund.

children.heading: Common and preferred stock investment
children.label: f Common and preferred stock investment

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An insured State bank shall not acquire or retain, directly or indirectly, any equity investment of a type or in an amount that is not permissible for a national bank or is not otherwise permitted under this section.

children.heading: Exception for banks in certain States
children.label: 2 Exception for banks in certain States
A
children.text: is located in a State that permitted, as of , investment in common or preferred stock listed on a national securities exchange or shares of an investment company registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 [ et seq.]; andSeptember 30, 199115 U.S.C. 80a–1

children.label: B
children.text: made or maintained an investment in such securities during the period beginning on , and ending on .September 30, 1990November 26, 1991

children.heading: Exception for certain types of institutions
children.label: 3 Exception for certain types of institutions
i
children.text: provides directors’, trustees’, and officers’ liability insurance coverage or bankers’ blanket bond group insurance coverage for insured depository institutions; or

children.label: ii
children.text: reinsures such policies; and

children.label: i
children.text: the institution engages only in activities permissible for national banks;

children.label: ii
children.text: the institution is subject to examination and regulation by a State bank supervisor;

children.label: iii
children.text: 20 or more depository institutions own shares of the institution and none of those institutions owns more than 15 percent of the institution’s shares; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the institution’s shares (other than directors’ qualifying shares or shares held under or initially acquired through a plan established for the benefit of the institution’s officers and employees) are owned only by the institution.

children.heading: Transition period for common and preferred stock investments
children.label: 4 Transition period for common and preferred stock investments

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: During each year in the 3-year period beginning on , each insured State bank shall reduce by not less than 1/3 of its shares (as of ) the bank’s ownership of securities in excess of the amount equal to 100 percent of the capital of such bank.December 19, 1991December 19, 1991

children.heading: Compliance at end of period
children.label: B Compliance at end of period
children.text: By the end of the 3-year period referred to in subparagraph (A), each insured State bank and each subsidiary of a State bank shall be in compliance with the maximum amount limitations on investments referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Loss of exception upon acquisition
children.label: 5 Loss of exception upon acquisition
children.text: Any exception applicable under paragraph (2) with respect to any insured State bank shall cease to apply with respect to such bank upon any change in control of such bank or any conversion of the charter of such bank.

children.label: A
children.text: the bank has filed a 1-time notice of the bank’s intention to acquire and retain investments described in paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: the Corporation has determined, within 60 days of receiving such notice, that acquiring or retaining such investments does not pose a significant risk to the Deposit Insurance Fund.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Corporation may require divestiture by an insured State bank of any investment permitted under this subsection if the Corporation determines that such investment will have an adverse effect on the safety and soundness of the bank.

children.heading: Reasonable standard
children.label: B Reasonable standard
children.text: The Corporation shall not require divestiture by any bank pursuant to subparagraph (A) without reason to believe that such investment will have an adverse effect on the safety and soundness of the bank.

children.heading: Determinations
children.label: g Determinations
children.text: The Corporation shall make determinations under this section by regulation or order.

children.heading: “Activity” defined
children.label: h “Activity” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “activity” includes acquiring or retaining any investment.

children.heading: Other authority not affected
children.label: i Other authority not affected
children.text: This section shall not be construed as limiting the authority of any appropriate Federal banking agency or any State supervisory authority to impose more stringent restrictions.

children.heading: Activities of branches of out-of-State banks
children.label: j Activities of branches of out-of-State banks

children.heading: Application of host State law
children.label: 1 Application of host State law
children.text: The laws of a host State, including laws regarding community reinvestment, consumer protection, fair lending, and establishment of intrastate branches, shall apply to any branch in the host State of an out-of-State State bank to the same extent as such State laws apply to a branch in the host State of an out-of-State national bank. To the extent host State law is inapplicable to a branch of an out-of-State State bank in such host State pursuant to the preceding sentence, home State law shall apply to such branch.

children.heading: Activities of branches
children.label: 2 Activities of branches
children.text: An insured State bank that establishes a branch in a host State may conduct any activity at such branch that is permissible under the laws of the home State of such bank, to the extent such activity is permissible either for a bank chartered by the host State (subject to the restrictions in this section) or for a branch in the host State of an out-of-State national bank.

children.label: A
children.text: any State law of any home State under subsection (b), (c), or (d) of ; orsection 1831u of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Federal law to State banks and State bank branches in the home State or the host State.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 4 Definitions
children.text: The terms “host State”, “home State”, and “out-of-State bank” have the same meanings as in section 1831u(f) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1
